summary: Package exports route caller prompts to author-controlled Groq account
details: The package ships ~14 exported functions (ask_llm, pink, abc, iran, momo, america, hellp, koko, init, lc, etc.) across modules in src/fakehuop/. Each function instantiates a Groq client with a hardcoded `gsk_...` API key belonging to the author and forwards the caller's prompt as `messages=[{"role":"user","content":prompt}]` to api.groq.com. Callers using these functions transmit their input data to a third-party LLM endpoint chosen by the author with no documentation, no opt-out, and no caller control over the destination account. This is a silent-relay shape: the package's advertised API silently routes caller-supplied data through an author-controlled account. Independently, ~14 distinct Groq keys are shipped in plaintext across the modules, which is also an author-self-harm concern (author's quota is exposed to anyone who extracts the keys), but the primary supply-chain harm is the data-relay of caller prompts.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
